frontend.label: Frontend
frontend.description: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and client-side web technologies

backend.label: Backend
backend.description: Python, Django, HTTP, and server-side web development

fe-0.description: Course logistics, web terminology, and how the syllabus progresses from protocols to frontend languages and Django.

coveredTopics.title: Course logistics
coveredTopics.summary: Grading, lecture norms, expectations, and reference materials.

coveredTopics.title: Web origins
coveredTopics.summary: Tim Berners-Lee's proposal at CERN and the birth of the World Wide Web.

coveredTopics.title: Web definitions
coveredTopics.summary: Static pages, websites, and interactive web applications.

coveredTopics.title: Dynamic websites
coveredTopics.summary: E-commerce, blogs, informational sites, and online communities.

coveredTopics.title: Course outline
coveredTopics.summary: Roadmap from protocols to HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Python, and Django.

fe-0.extent: High-level introductory overview with foundational definitions and a course roadmap.

fe-1.description: Internet protocol layers with a deep dive into HTTP mechanics, caching, cookies, and web security.

coveredTopics.title: TCP/IP layers
coveredTopics.summary: Application, transport, internet, and network interface layers.

coveredTopics.title: Core protocols
coveredTopics.summary: TCP, IP, HTTP, and SMTP definitions and roles.

coveredTopics.title: HTTP fundamentals
coveredTopics.summary: URLs, media types, connections, caching, and cookies.

coveredTopics.title: HTTP requests
coveredTopics.summary: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE methods and common request headers.

coveredTopics.title: HTTP responses
coveredTopics.summary: Status code categories (1xx–5xx) and response headers.

coveredTopics.title: Caching & cookies
coveredTopics.summary: Time-based vs content-based caching, limits, and security risks.

coveredTopics.title: Security
coveredTopics.summary: Authentication methods, authorization (403), and HTTPS.

fe-1.extent: Comprehensive breakdown of HTTP request/response structures and authentication methods.

fe-2.description: Web architecture fundamentals and standard HTML/HTML5—from document structure to modern browser storage.

coveredTopics.summary: Client–browser–server model and ports 80 (HTTP) and 443 (HTTPS).

coveredTopics.summary: Headings, paragraphs, lists, tables, links, and images.

coveredTopics.summary: Bold, italic, subscript, superscript, deleted, and inserted text.

coveredTopics.title: Special characters
coveredTopics.summary: HTML entity encoding for ampersands, arrows, and symbols.

coveredTopics.title: HTML5 features
coveredTopics.summary: New input types, offline caching, localStorage, and sessionStorage.

fe-2.extent: Practical guide to HTML tags, document structure, and modern browser storage.

fe-3.description: How JavaScript navigates, modifies, and manages HTML elements through the DOM.

coveredTopics.summary: Hierarchical nodes and parent, child, and sibling relationships.

coveredTopics.summary: createElement, appendChild, removeChild, and replaceChild.

coveredTopics.title: Modern DOM APIs
coveredTopics.summary: classList, dataset, and addEventListener.

coveredTopics.title: Performance
coveredTopics.summary: Minimizing reflows with DocumentFragment and related techniques.

fe-3.extent: Detailed exploration of DOM traversal, manipulation, modern APIs, and performance.

fe-4.description: CSS syntax, selectors, layout, responsive design, and modern styling from the box model to Grid and Flexbox.

coveredTopics.summary: Inline, internal, and external stylesheets plus cascade priority.

coveredTopics.summary: Backgrounds, text colors (Hex, RGB, HSL), and borders.

coveredTopics.title: The box model
coveredTopics.summary: Margin and padding control on each side of an element.

coveredTopics.summary: Media queries, CSS Grid, and mobile-first layouts.

coveredTopics.summary: Custom properties, Flexbox, transitions, and specificity.

fe-5.description: Core JavaScript syntax, types, operators, functions, built-in objects, basic DOM/events, and OOP fundamentals.

coveredTopics.title: Data types & variables
coveredTopics.summary: Numbers, booleans, strings, null, undefined, scoping, and coercion.

coveredTopics.title: Operators
coveredTopics.summary: Arithmetic, logical, delete, in, typeof, and void.

coveredTopics.summary: Function syntax, arguments, try/catch, and eval risks.

fe-6.description: Modern JavaScript from ES5 through ES11—array methods, classes, async/await—and an introduction to AJAX.

coveredTopics.summary: Strict mode, string/array methods, JSON, Date formatting, getters/setters.

coveredTopics.summary: forEach, map, filter, reduce, every, some, indexOf, lastIndexOf, and find.

coveredTopics.summary: let, const, arrow functions, classes, promises, defaults, and rest parameters.

coveredTopics.summary: includes, exponentiation, padStart/padEnd, async/await, object spread, and Promise.finally.

coveredTopics.summary: flat, flatMap, and private class fields.

coveredTopics.summary: XMLHttpRequest, asynchronous requests, and the client–server cycle.

fe-6.extent: Detailed ES5–ES11 features and array methods, plus a brief AJAX introduction.
